steps.title: Fundamental property of real squares
steps.content: For any real number $x$, the square $x^2$ is always non-negative. This means $x^2 \geq 0$ for all real $x$.

steps.title: Apply to the equation
steps.content: The equation requires $x^2 = -841$. Since $-841$ is negative and $x^2$ must be non-negative for all real values of $x$, there is no real number that satisfies this equation. Therefore, there are zero real solutions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
